title: Easter biscuits
subtitle: Spiced currant shortbread from the West Country
excerpt: Easter biscuits are large, flat, lightly spiced shortbread-style biscuits studded with currants and flavoured with mixed spice and a little lemon zest. They are a West Country tradition, eaten at Easter. The texture is crisp at the edges and slightly soft in the centre when freshly baked, firming up completely as they cool.
sourceNotes: Easter biscuits are a traditional English West Country recipe, particularly associated with Bristol and Somerset. Recipes appear in English regional cookery collections from the nineteenth century. The spiced currant composition is consistent: a shortcrust base flavoured with mixed spice, cinnamon, or caraway, enriched with currants, and baked large, which distinguishes them from the smaller currant biscuits found elsewhere. The lemon zest is a modern addition.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Store in an airtight tin for up to 5 days.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; 12 to 15 minutes until the edges are pale gold and the top is dry

content.text: Easter biscuits are cut larger than most stamped biscuits: use a 7 to 8 cm round cutter. The dough is a simple rubbed-in shortcrust, enriched with egg yolk to give a slightly richer flavour. Chilling the cut biscuits before baking helps them hold their shape. They should not colour deeply; the target is a pale, sandy gold at the edge with a dry, set surface.

items.prepNote: yolk only, plus 1 tsp cold water

content.text: Sift 200 g plain flour and 1 tsp mixed spice into a large bowl. Add 100 g cold diced butter and rub in until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs. Stir in 100 g caster sugar, 75 g currants, and the zest of 1 lemon. Add 1 egg yolk mixed with 1 teaspoon of cold water and bring together into a smooth dough. If it is too dry, add another teaspoon of water. Wrap and refrigerate for 20 minutes.

Heat the oven to 180 degrees C fan. Line two baking trays with baking paper. Roll the dough on a lightly floured surface to about 5 mm thick. Cut out rounds with a 7 to 8 cm cutter and place on the trays. Re-roll the trimmings once. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes until the edges are pale gold and the surface feels dry. Sprinkle with caster sugar while warm. Leave on the tray for 10 minutes to firm, then transfer to a rack.

items.solution: The dough was too warm when it went into the oven. Chill the cut biscuits on the tray in the fridge for 15 minutes before baking.
They needed more time in the oven. The edges should be a definite pale gold, not white. Return to a 160 degrees C oven for a further 5 minutes and cool on the tray.
